Lawn mowing prices in Guelph (2026)

Most companies make you call to find out. Here’s our honest guide to what mowing costs in Guelph — including our own prices.

Get a Free Quote Call or Text
Our pricing

Yardex weekly mowing starts at

$27.50 per cut

That’s the real starting price for smaller Guelph city lots — and unlike most quotes you’ll get, it’s the whole job: mowing, trimming around obstacles, edging as needed, and a full blowdown of your driveway and walkways. Larger properties, corner lots, and yards with lots of obstacles are quoted individually, and the price we quote is the price you pay all season.

What moves the price up or down
FactorWhy it matters
Lawn sizeThe biggest factor — more grass, more time
ObstaclesBeds, trees and fences add trimming time
Backyard accessNarrow gates mean smaller mowers and more passes
SlopesSteep sections are slower and cut with extra care
ConditionOvergrown first cuts take longer than maintained lawns
Get your exact price — free
Market context

What mowing costs in Ontario — and how to spot a fair quote

Across Ontario, professional residential mowing commonly runs somewhere between $50 and $100 per visit, with hourly landscape rates higher still. Guelph sits below big-city GTA pricing, but most established companies here still quote well above our range — and the franchises typically bundle mowing into seasonal programs with prepay discounts and auto-renewal.

Too cheap is also a warning sign

You’ll find $25 cash offers on Kijiji with no insurance, borrowed equipment, and no promise of showing up next week. A fair quote should come from someone who can tell you exactly what’s included, arrives with commercial equipment, and stands behind the work with a visible public reputation.

How we’re priced below the established companies

No franchise fees, no office lease, no sales team — Yardex is a student-built team of 5, and our overhead reflects it. That’s the honest reason our prices are lower. The equipment, the schedule, and the finish are the parts we don’t compromise, which is how we’ve earned 75+ five-star Google reviews doing it.

Weekly service math

A Guelph mowing season runs roughly late April to late October — about 24 to 26 weekly visits, minus any skips in dry spells. At our starting rate, that’s a full season of never touching a mower for less than many companies charge for a prepaid “basic program.” And because there’s no contract, you can stop whenever you like and owe nothing further.

Pricing questions

Frequently asked

Why do you publish prices when other companies don’t?

Because “call for a quote” usually means “we price based on what we think you’ll pay.” Publishing our starting price keeps us honest and saves you time. Your exact quote is still free and takes under 24 hours.

Is $27.50 a teaser rate that jumps later?

No. It’s the real per-cut price for smaller Guelph city lots, and your quoted price is the price all season. The only thing that changes it is you asking for more services.

Do I pay less if I commit to the whole season?

You don’t need to commit at all — that’s the point. Weekly clients simply get priority scheduling. There are no prepay programs and nothing to enroll in.

How do you decide the price for my lawn?

Lawn size is the biggest factor, then obstacles (beds, trees, fencing, slopes) and gate access for backyards. Send your address and we’ll quote it precisely — we know most Guelph streets already.

What payment methods do you take?

E-transfer is easiest for most clients; we’ll confirm current options with your quote. Invoices are clear and itemized — no surprise line items.

See exactly what’s in every visit on our lawn mowing page, or check what Guelph homeowners say about the work.

Get your exact price

Send your address — we already know most Guelph streets and can usually quote same-day.
